label: Ego-graph orientation layer on PC skeleton
grounding_detail: MosaCD [#1], chatPC [#4], CausalFusion [#2], CauScientist [#3] leave local ego-graph middle ground unexplored. V2 proposal narrows claim to orientation-layer operating point.

label: PC-stable skeleton backend (Phase 0)
grounding_detail: PC-stable [Colombo & Maathuis 2014] is order-independent and standard in LLM-causal papers. CPC as robustness variant.

label: Ego-graph prompting as LLM interface (Phase 1)
grounding_detail: Core thesis: ego-graph is richer than per-edge (exposes phi consistency potential) yet bounded (O(d) context). MVE will test empirically. Degree cap at 8.

label: Local hard-constraint compilation / Max-2SAT (Phase 2)
grounding_detail: CI-derived collider/non-collider constraints form 2-SAT clauses. Exact enumeration feasible for d<=8. LLM soft scores as objective. Infeasibility policy pre-specified.

label: Dual-endpoint Dawid-Skene reconciliation (Phase 3)
grounding_detail: Dawid-Skene [1979] for latent-label aggregation. Each edge gets 2+ judgments from endpoint egos. Borrowed module, not a contribution. EBCC sensitivity mandatory.

label: Conservative propagation + Meek closure (Phase 4)
grounding_detail: Meek [1995] rules are sound and complete for CPDAG orientation. Conservative scheduling inspired by decimation literature. Rollback safeguards for early commitments.

label: Budgeted per-edge fallback (Phase 5)
grounding_detail: MosaCD-style per-edge queries reserved for hard tail (~10-15% of edges). Budget-capped to preserve selective output.

label: Venn-Abers calibration + selective output (Phase 6)
grounding_detail: Multiclass Venn-Abers [Vovk et al. 2004, 2025]. Edge-level selective output with calibrated confidence. Formal claims restricted to exchangeable graph instances only.

label: Confirmatory benchmark suite
grounding_detail: Asia, Sachs, Alarm, Insurance (overlap with MosaCD/chatPC). 100-node ER and scale-free synthetic. Oracle + estimated skeletons. Descriptions + anonymized metadata.

label: Orientation-only primary evaluation mode
grounding_detail: V2 scopes primary claim to orientation quality given fixed skeleton. End-to-end is secondary. Per audit C01 recommendation.

label: EBCC/BCC dependence-aware reconciliation
grounding_detail: Endpoint egos share LLM and variable descriptions -> not independent. EBCC [Kim & Ghahramani 2012] handles correlated annotators. Mandatory sensitivity check on confirmatory subset.

label: Hub-degree splitting strategy
grounding_detail: Nodes with degree > 8 need sub-ego splitting. Options: graph clustering, sepset-informed grouping, overlapping sub-egos. Details TBD.

label: 200-node scaling experiments
grounding_detail: Budget-dependent. Feasibility audit estimates $150-400+ for 200-node runs. Appendix only unless budget allows.

label: Real-world large graph (e.g., Neuropathic Pain 221-node)
grounding_detail: Metadata curation is the blocker. Feasibility audit flags as likely blocker for v1. Appendix-only or drop.

label: Metadata leakage control protocol
grounding_detail: Need standardized description protocol with provenance and scrub pass removing causal language. Critical for anonymized condition. Details TBD.

label: LLM-based skeleton refinement (recover PC-missed edges)
rejected: pairwise LLM edge queries for missing edges
rationale: Three independent root causes: (1) most GT-missing edges are >2 hops apart in skeleton (unreachable), (2) LLM too conservative on genuinely ambiguous edges, (3) approach outside proposal scope (proposal says LLM does not alter skeleton). Negative result strengthens F1 decomposition contribution.

label: MosaCD re-implementation for same-model fair comparison
decision: Re-implement MosaCD's 5-step algorithm from paper (no public repo) using same LLM (Qwen3.5-27B) and same skeleton (PC-stable)
rejected: use reported MosaCD numbers directly (different model, different skeleton)
reproduce with GPT-4o-mini (different model confounds comparison)
rationale: Isolates methodological difference (ego-graph+NCO vs per-edge+shuffled-debiasing+propagation) from model difference. Paper provides complete algorithm, all 4 prompt templates, and parsing regex.

label: Iterative BFS ego-graph expansion (SP-style decimation)
decision: Negative result: single-pass K=10 beats multi-round K=5x2 on Insurance (-6.3pp F1), ties on Alarm. More votes > more context.
rationale: Original proposal design from raw_idea.md. Tested but iterative context enrichment does not improve over single-pass. Error amplification from early decimation and fewer votes per round are the root causes.

label: Phase transition DO→THINK after multi-seed validation
decision: Multi-seed validation (4 seeds × 5 networks × 2 methods) complete. LOCALE 4W/0T/1L vs MosaCD. research-reflect approved with high confidence.
rejected: stay in DO for more experiments (cross-model, disguised multi-seed)
rationale: Multi-seed was the explicit prerequisite (LOG-2026-03-11-32). Results exceeded expectations (4W/0T/1L vs 1W/3T single-seed). Additional experiments can loop back from THINK if needed.

label: Narrative reframe: NCO is the cargo, ego-graph is the vehicle
decision: Paper narrative should lead with NCO discovery (97.9% false colliders) as the surprise finding, with ego-graph batching as the method that exploits it. Previous diagnostic framing from 2W/3T era replaced.
rationale: Narrator agent analysis: NCO finding is generalizable beyond LOCALE, theoretically grounded (MosaCD Thm 5.5), and empirically validated at scale (944 errors). Ego-graph is the vehicle but NCO is what makes LOCALE work. Judge confirmed: must cite MosaCD Thm 5.5, report paired significance tests, disclose Alarm context overflow.

label: Phase transition THINK→DO: skeleton improvement + MosaCD replication
decision: Loop back to DO to address two critical gaps before SAY: (1) MosaCD comparison is confounded by 4096-token context overflow and model gap — need faithful replication, (2) skeleton identified as binding constraint but unaddressed — need systematic exploration
rejected: proceed to SAY with current results (reviewer: contribution is thin, 2/4 wins not significant, comparison baseline suspect)
rationale: User directive: explore all directions before paper. Reviewer found MosaCD re-implementation may systematically disadvantage MosaCD (context overflow on Alarm, unvalidated against published numbers). research-reflect priority ordering: (1) MosaCD replication, (2) cross-model comparison, (3) skeleton improvement. Both agents agree SAY is premature.

label: NCO-informed skeleton improvement (statistical, not LLM-based)
decision: Alpha tuning helps Asia only (87.5%→100% at alpha=0.10). Insurance stuck at 80.8% (PC depth limit). Multi-alpha union adds no value beyond single alpha. Skeleton improvement via alpha is a dead end for the hardest networks.
rejected: accept skeleton as-is (F1 decomposition shows it is THE bottleneck)
rationale: XN-028: tested 4 alphas (0.01-0.20) on 5 networks. Insurance hits PC conditioning depth limit regardless of alpha. Asia is the only network that benefits (1 missing edge recovered at alpha=0.10). CPC and alternative CI tests remain untested but unlikely to overcome the depth limit. Practical impact: use alpha=0.10 for Asia to recover 100% skeleton coverage.

label: MosaCD faithful replication: 12-seed fair comparison at 4096 context
decision: 12-seed comparison complete. LOCALE wins Insurance (p=0.008) and Sachs (p<0.0001), loses Asia (p<0.0001), ties Alarm and Child (directional but ns after Holm). Also discovered MosaCD context sensitivity (XN-029): breaks at 2048 tokens.
rationale: Ran all 12 seeds at 4096 context after discovering the 2048-context confound (XN-029). Insurance gained significance (was ns at 4 seeds). Alarm lost Holm significance (MosaCD variance increased with more seeds). Context sensitivity is itself a contribution: LOCALE is 2-3x more context-efficient.

label: Data seed bug: LOCALE used fixed seed=42 for all multi-seed runs
decision: sample_data() default parameter bound at definition time (seed=42). Fix: pass seed=SEED_DATA explicitly. All 12-seed LOCALE results invalidated and must be re-run.
rationale: LOCALE always generated data with seed=42 regardless of --seed flag. MosaCD correctly varied data per seed. This meant: (1) LOCALE skeletons were identical across seeds, (2) LOCALE variance was artificially low (only LLM variance, not data+LLM), (3) paired comparison unfair (different skeletons for same seed). Bug in mve_insurance.py line 1154: sample_data(model, n=N_SAMPLES) used default seed=42 instead of explicit seed=SEED_DATA.

reason: 12-seed comparison invalidated by data seed bug (A02). Re-running all LOCALE experiments with correct per-seed data generation.
Re-run complete with corrected data seeds. Headline unchanged: 2 SIG wins + 2 directional + 1 SIG loss (Holm-corrected). XN-031 supersedes XN-030.

label: Phase transition DO→THINK after corrected 12-seed comparison
decision: Transition to THINK. Corrected comparison (XN-031) validates headline: 2 SIG wins + 1 SIG loss. Previous loop-back concerns (data seed bug, context confound, insufficient seeds) all resolved. research-reflect approved with medium-high confidence.
rejected: stay in DO for synthetic experiments (proposal Section 5.5)
stay in DO for more disguised robustness seeds
rationale: The critical confounds that triggered PT-04 are resolved. Remaining gaps (synthetic, Hepar2, cross-model) are either diminishing returns or evaluatable in THINK. THINK evaluates publishability — it can loop back to DO if threats-to-validity gates (Section 5.7) are not met.
